# Safety Analytics: Elevate Workplace Safety with Data Insights

![](https://www.sitedocs.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/Blog-Image-32.webp "Blog-Image-32")

In today’s fast-paced and complex work environments, safety managers and risk mitigation professionals need cutting-edge tools to stay ahead of potential hazards. Enter safety analytics—a powerful ally in leveraging data to proactively mitigate risks.

This blog post will guide you through the intricate world of safety analytics, focusing on predictive modeling, trend analysis, and how to use tools like SiteDocs Analytics to transform data into actionable insights.

### The Power of Safety Analytics for Risk Mitigation

Safety analytics is revolutionizing how organizations approach workplace safety. By harnessing vast amounts of data, safety managers can identify potential hazards before they become incidents. This proactive approach not only protects employees but also enhances operational efficiency.

One of the most significant benefits of safety analytics is the ability to make data-driven decisions. Instead of relying on intuition or past experiences, safety managers can utilize real-time data to assess risks accurately. This shift from reactive to proactive risk management is a game-changer for industries where safety is paramount.

Additionally, safety analytics provides a comprehensive view of an organization’s safety performance. With tools like SiteDocs Analytics, safety managers can visualize data through interactive dashboards, making it easier to spot trends and correlations that might otherwise go unnoticed.

### Predictive Analytics vs. Descriptive Analytics

When discussing safety analytics, it’s essential to differentiate between predictive and descriptive analytics. Both play crucial roles in risk mitigation but serve different purposes.

Descriptive Analytics focuses on understanding what has happened in the past. By analyzing historical data, safety managers can identify patterns and trends that have led to previous incidents. This information is invaluable for developing strategies to prevent similar occurrences in the future.

Predictive Analytics, on the other hand, looks forward. This proactive approach allows safety managers to implement preventive measures, significantly reducing the likelihood of incidents.  
Both types of analytics are integral to a robust safety strategy. Descriptive analytics provides the foundation of knowledge, while predictive analytics empowers organizations to anticipate and mitigate future risks.

### Identifying Leading Indicators

Leading indicators are metrics that predict future safety performance. Unlike lagging indicators, which reflect past incidents, leading indicators provide early warning signs of potential problems. Identifying and monitoring these indicators is crucial for proactive risk mitigation.

Common leading indicators include near-miss reports, safety training completion rates, and equipment maintenance schedules. By tracking these metrics, safety managers can identify areas of concern before they escalate into significant issues.

Tools like SiteDocs Analytics make it easy to monitor leading indicators. With customizable dashboards and detailed reporting features, safety managers can gain deep insights into their organization’s safety performance and take corrective actions as needed.

### Continuous Improvement Strategies

Safety analytics is not a one-time solution; it’s an ongoing process. Continuous improvement strategies are essential for maintaining a high level of workplace safety. By regularly analyzing data and assessing the effectiveness of safety measures, organizations can refine their strategies and achieve better outcomes.

One effective approach is to conduct regular safety audits. These audits provide a comprehensive review of safety practices and identify areas for improvement. By integrating audit results with safety analytics, organizations can develop targeted action plans to address specific issues.

Another strategy is to foster a culture of safety within the organization. Encouraging employees to report near-misses and participate in safety training can provide valuable data for safety analytics. This collaborative approach ensures that everyone is invested in maintaining a safe work environment.

### Using SiteDocs Analytics for Safety Management

SiteDocs Analytics is a powerful tool designed to enhance safety management through data visualization, trend analysis, and Analytics-driven insights. Let’s explore how this platform can transform your organization’s approach to safety.

#### Data Visualization

SiteDocs Analytics provides interactive dashboards with various charts and diagrams, making it easy to visualize safety data. Whether you’re tracking incident reports, training completion rates, or equipment maintenance schedules, these visualizations provide a clear and concise overview of your organization’s safety performance.

#### Trend Analysis

Spotting trends and evaluating relationships between data sets is crucial for proactive risk mitigation. SiteDocs Analytics allows safety managers to identify patterns and correlations that may indicate potential risks. By analyzing historical data, organizations can develop strategies to prevent future incidents.

#### Custom Filtering

Every organization is unique, and so are its safety needs. SiteDocs Analytics offers custom filtering options, allowing safety managers to focus on the precise information they need. Whether you’re analyzing data by location, department, or specific safety metrics, this feature ensures that you have the insights required to make informed decisions.

#### Pre-built and Custom Dashboards

SiteDocs Analytics includes several pre-built dashboards out of the box, providing immediate value to safety managers. Additionally, users can build unlimited custom dashboards tailored to their organization’s specific needs. This flexibility ensures that you can track the metrics that matter most to your safety program.

#### Detailed Reporting

Building detailed reports from individual questions and answers in custom forms is a breeze with SiteDocs Analytics. These reports provide deep insights into specific safety issues, allowing organizations to address them effectively. Whether you’re preparing for a safety audit or evaluating the effectiveness of training programs, detailed reporting is a valuable asset.

#### AI Insights

Artificial intelligence plays a significant role in SiteDocs Analytics. The platform uses AI to review charts and provide natural language insights, ensuring that safety managers understand the data quickly and accurately. This advanced technology empowers organizations to make data-driven decisions with confidence.

#### Data Sharing

Effective communication is essential for maintaining a safe work environment. SiteDocs Analytics enables data sharing through downloadable dashboards, scheduled emails, and shared access within the organization. This feature ensures that everyone is on the same page and can collaborate effectively to improve safety.

### Conclusion

Tools like SiteDocs Analytics provide the insights and capabilities needed to transform raw data into actionable strategies. From data visualization and trend analysis to Analytics-driven insights and automatic monitoring, these platforms empower organizations to stay ahead of risks and continuously improve their safety programs.

Don’t wait for the next incident to take action. Start leveraging safety analytics today and create a safer, more efficient work environment.

By adopting a proactive approach to safety, you’re not only protecting your employees but also enhancing your organization’s overall performance. Let’s make workplace safety a top priority—together.

# KPIs for Safety for Visualizing Safety Progress

KPIs for safety—why should they matter to your business? Metrics provide clarity, exposing risk before it turns into injury. It helps you spot trends and keeps your team accountable.

![KPIs for Safety](https://chartexpo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/kpis-for-safety-main.jpg)
![](https://chartexpo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/CTA-in-google-sheets-1.jpg)
![](https://chartexpo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/CTA-in-microsoft-excel-1.jpg)

Without clear targets, teams operate on guesswork. With KPIs for Safety, you get precision. You know how many days have passed since the last incident. You see which departments report near misses and identify where training gaps exist. These aren’t vague insights—they’re facts you can act on.

Let’s face it—compliance isn’t enough, but performance matters. Leading companies track customer success KPIs and KPIs for call center operations. Why treat safety any differently? Numbers drive action across every department; therefore, safety should be no exception.

Today’s high-performing organizations are data-driven. Safety is part of the bigger picture. Executives diligently monitor KPI graphs for finance and customer service KPI dashboards; safety deserves that same focus.

Safety isn’t a checklist. It’s a culture, and KPIs shape that culture. They keep teams honest, and most importantly, they save lives. That’s why KPIs aren’t paperwork—they’re protection.

Start measuring what matters. Because what you track, you improve. And what you improve, you keep safe.

Are you ready to make the numbers speak?

Follow my lead…

### Table of Contents:

## What are Safety KPIs?

**Definition:** Safety KPIs are key performance indicators that measure a company’s effectiveness in managing safety. They measure incidents, risks, and safety actions. These KPIs track trends in injuries, near misses, and training, enabling companies to prevent accidents.

KPIs for safety help spot problems early and improve accountability. Examples include lost-time injury rate and incident frequency, leading to better safety decisions. Safety KPIs turn data into action. [SMART KPIs](https://chartexpo.com/blog/smart-kpis-examples) ensure goals are specific and measurable for safer outcomes.

## Why Use Safety Key Performance Indicators?

Every accident prevented is a win. But wins don’t happen by chance—they’re tracked, measured, and repeated. That’s the power of using KPIs for safety. They turn daily operations into [data-driven decisions](https://chartexpo.com/blog/data-driven-decision-making) that protect people and drive performance. Here’s why they matter:

## Top 10 Important Safety KPIs

Every workplace has risks. But smart ones track those risks with purpose. That’s where KPIs for safety lead the way. They give real-time insight into what’s working—and what needs attention. Here are 10 powerful KPIs every safety-focused company should track:

## How to Analyze a Safety KPI Template in Excel?

Why use KPIs for safety? Simple: safety metrics shouldn’t be buried in a spreadsheet, gathering dust. Excel does its job, but when it comes to visualizing complex safety KPIs, it falls short. Pie charts and [bar graphs](https://chartexpo.com/blog/bar-graph) only go so far.

Enter ChartExpo: the game-changer for KPIs for safety. It turns raw data into powerful visuals that are easy to understand and act on. No more squinting at numbers—ChartExpo makes [data visualization](https://chartexpo.com/blog/data-visualization-guide) a breeze, helping you confidently track [metrics and KPIs](https://chartexpo.com/blog/metrics-and-kpis).

**How to Install ChartExpo in Excel?**

ChartExpo charts are available both in Google Sheets and Microsoft Excel. Please use the following CTAs to install the tool of your choice and create beautiful visualizations with a few clicks in your favorite tool.

#### Insights

## Tips for Tracking Safety KPIs

Tracking safety metrics isn’t about filling in spreadsheets. It’s about saving lives, reducing risk, and making smarter daily decisions. If you’re going to track KPIs for safety, you need to do it purposefully. Numbers should lead to action, not collect dust, and here’s how to make your tracking count:

![](https://chartexpo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/CTA-in-google-sheets-3.jpg)
![](https://chartexpo.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/CTA-in-microsoft-excel-3.jpg)

## FAQs

### What are the leading indicators of safety?

Leading indicators are proactive safety measures. They track actions taken to prevent incidents. Examples include training sessions, safety meetings, and near-miss reports. These indicators help spot risks early and improve workplace safety before harm occurs.

### What are the key risk indicators in safety?

Key risk indicators show potential threats to safety. They reflect conditions or behaviors that may lead to incidents. Examples include overdue inspections or rising near-miss rates. These signals help teams act before accidents happen and reduce risk exposure.

### How to measure safety performance?

#### Wrap Up

KPIs for safety indicators are more than numbers. They help you protect people by showing where risks live. This way, they push you and your team to act before accidents happen.

Safety doesn’t improve on guesswork. It improves through tracking and action. When you measure what matters, you can fix what’s broken. Data creates focus.

Shortcuts in safety cost lives. Using metrics and KPIs helps keep standards high, ensuring nothing slips through the cracks. You catch problems early, not after they hurt someone.

Excel can track data, but it can’t always explain it. You need to see the story behind the stats. That’s where data visualization matters, with tools like ChartExpo shining. With clear visuals, trends make sense, leaders make better choices, and teams feel more accountable. Whether measuring KPIs for call center efficiency or evaluating what sales KPIs are, the goal is the same—action through insight.

Safety KPIs drive results—fewer injuries, more awareness, stronger systems, and safer workplaces for everyone. Don’t rely on chance—measure, monitor, and act.

And for better results? Install ChartExpo to visualize your data and take your safety to the next level.

URL: https://help.safetyculture.com/en-US/001032/

SUMMARY:
They allow you to visualize key trends by changing chart types, adjusting attributes, and changing filters. You can also show multiple charts on your dashboard.

FULL CONTENT:
![SafetyCulture](/logo/sc-logo-color.svg)

# Analytics chart customization options

## What are Analytics charts?

[Analytics charts](/en-US/001403/ "Add charts") are visual tools you can add to your [Analytics dashboards](/en-US/001997/ "Create Analytics dashboards") to display the data that matter most to you and your organization. They allow you to visualize key trends by [changing chart types](/en-US/004505/ "Change chart types"), [adjusting attributes](/en-US/004502/ "Configure chart metrics and attributes"), and [changing filters](/en-US/000005/ "Apply Analytics filters"). You can also show multiple charts on your dashboard so your team can gain insights from multiple data sources at a glance.

We're constantly working to expand and enhance chart customization options for you. If you have any feedback or suggestions, we'd love to [hear them from you](https://safetyculture.com/product-feedback/ "hear them from you")!

## Chart customization options

This option allows you to select [metrics and related attributes](/en-US/004502/ "Configure chart metrics and attributes") depending on the type of data you want to report on, whether it's for inspections, actions, or issues.

Press to play GIF

This option lets you [add chart titles](/en-US/003907/ "Add chart titles") so you can easily identify the data that your charts represent.

![Edit your Analytics chart titles via the SafetyCulture web app.](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fwum34wy9buzj%2F3GloEOEB5GphH2pmPs8LV0%2F4a9e954ab38bad37829444ec863943eb%2FSafetyCulture__1_.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Edit your Analytics chart titles via the SafetyCulture web app.](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fwum34wy9buzj%2F3GloEOEB5GphH2pmPs8LV0%2F4a9e954ab38bad37829444ec863943eb%2FSafetyCulture__1_.png&w=3840&q=75)

This option allows you to select a [chart type](/en-US/004505/ "Change chart types") that visually represents your data. Based on your needs, you can choose a bar, pie, line, table, dial, or dual-axis chart.

Press to play GIF

This option allows you to add a [key performance indicator (KPI)](/en-US/003908/ "Configure chart KPIs") and a target value to show whether your organization has reached a particular goal.

![Set and monitor your organization's goals by adding in a KPI for your Analytics chart via the SafetyCulture web app.](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fwum34wy9buzj%2F3bLgHjCH11V3YZ6CZE84kL%2Fca568cfe03723128c52d957c8fea17a6%2FSafetyCulture__2_.png&w=3840&q=75)

![Set and monitor your organization's goals by adding in a KPI for your Analytics chart via the SafetyCulture web app.](/_next/image/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fwum34wy9buzj%2F3bLgHjCH11V3YZ6CZE84kL%2Fca568cfe03723128c52d957c8fea17a6%2FSafetyCulture__2_.png&w=3840&q=75)

This option lets you [control which data to show or hide](/en-US/004710/ "Customize Analytics chart data visibility") in your charts, reducing noise and ensuring that they only show relevant details.

Press to play GIF

This option lets you r[esize your charts](/en-US/001943/ "Resize Analytics charts") to appear smaller or larger on your dashboards so you can better see which data is most relevant to your organization.

Press to play GIF

Selecting metrics under different data types is not currently supported for dual-axis charts. For example, only metrics under the Inspections data set can be used in a dual-axis chart.

## Frequently asked questions

No, it's currently not possible to customize and select different colors for your charts.

Charts use a set of 12 default colors, applied in order from the metric with the most data to the least. The color order is: SafetyCulture purple, yellow, light blue, purple, red, green, light orange, blue, orange, light green, turquoise, and pink. Only the first 15 data points are displayed individually, and any remaining data is grouped together and shown in gray.

# Enhancing Aviation Safety: Expert Insights, Tips, and Updates from SMS-Pro

# 8 Charts to Monitor Safety Culture Performance in Safety Management Systems (SMS)

![](https://aviationsafetyblog.asms-pro.com/hubfs/images/HowellSmsPro145w.png)

## Types of Aviation Safety Culture

![8 Charts to Monitor Safety Culture Performance in Safety Management Systems (SMS)](https://aviationsafetyblog.asms-pro.com/hubfs/images/Blog_Images/8-Charts-to-Monitor-aviation-Safety-Culture-Performance-in-safety-management-systems.png "8 Charts to Monitor Safety Culture Performance in Safety Management Systems (SMS)")

While it is normal to say that safety culture is ***something*** you should pay attention to, it might be better to say that safety culture ***are things*** you should pay attention to.

**Safety culture in aviation organizations** is more than just one thing. There are [multiple “types” of safety culture](/blog/6-types-of-safety-culture-in-aviation-safety-management-system).

The five faces of safety culture you should consider are:

You cannot monitor aviation safety culture with one safety chart or one metric. You need a handful of dedicated charts to acquire a global picture of aviation safety culture in your aviation safety management system.

[![Take Safety Culture](https://no-cache.hubspot.com/cta/default/543594/2f65ea95-24e4-45ef-b97c-0f58a665efe2.png)](https://cta-redirect.hubspot.com/cta/redirect/543594/2f65ea95-24e4-45ef-b97c-0f58a665efe2)

![Take Safety Culture](https://no-cache.hubspot.com/cta/default/543594/2f65ea95-24e4-45ef-b97c-0f58a665efe2.png)

## 1 – Chart to Monitor Safety Culture Commitment and Attitude

Commitment and attitude are really two sides of the same coin. When we talk about safety commitment, it is usually in the context of management’s commitment to the safety program:

It should be clear whether or not your management is committed to the safety program. However, the safety attitude of front-line employees is harder to qualify/quantify. Employee safety attitudes are things like:

These are important questions. Organizations with employees who have good safety attitudes are safer organizations, period. The best way to assess employees’ attitudes is with safety surveys. By taking surveys, you can chart the results onto a **safety survey results graph**. Over time, you will be able to assess the trend in safety attitudes. This is a good baseline for assessing the effectiveness of your safety promotion efforts.

## 2 – Chart to Monitor Safety Culture Behavior

![Policies and procedures classifications chart](https://aviationsafetyblog.asms-pro.com/hubfs/images/Blog_Images/Procedures%20Classifications%20chart.jpg "Policies and procedures classifications chart")

What are the employees in your safety management system doing when the boss isn’t watching? Are they following prescribed safety procedures? Employees who take shortcuts with tasks or prescribed procedures are unsafe. Safety management’s safety efforts depend on the assumption that employees are following prescribed behavior.

There are three important parts of safety behavior:

A great chart to assess this is the **policies and procures classification chart**. This chart shows which policies and procedures are implicated in safety issues. If a policy should be working, but it keeps being classified, it means either:

A great chart to use in conjunction is the **top [Human Factors](https://www.skybrary.aero/index.php/Company_Safety_Culture_(OGHFA_BN)) classification chart**. If you find that employees not following directions is a significant contributor, then you know what your problem is.

## 3 – Chart to Monitor Hazard Reporting Culture (and Just Culture)

![Hazard reporting activity over time chart](https://aviationsafetyblog.asms-pro.com/hubfs/images/Blog_Images/Hazard%20reporting%20activity%20over%20time%20chart.jpg "Hazard reporting activity over time chart")

Hazard reporting culture is your baseline safety culture performance. If employees aren’t reporting issues, your safety program is a farce. Even if your SMS is riddled with other problems, so long as employees are submitting safety issues you can practice continuous improvement.

There are two charts you can use to monitor safety reporting culture:

These two charts give you an opportunity to assess the global hazard reporting culture and hazard reporting of each employee. Poor hazard reporting cultures can be in part a "human" problem, as well as a [hazard reporting process](/blog/what-good-hazard-reporting-process-looks-like-in-real-sms-program) problem. Other charts that are useful for assessing hazard reporting culture are:

## 4 – Chart to Monitor Awareness

![Hazard reports by risk level chart](https://aviationsafetyblog.asms-pro.com/hubfs/images/Blog_Images/Hazard%20reports%20by%20risk%20level%20chart.jpg "Hazard reports by risk level chart")

Instilling high-quality safety awareness may be the highest goal of a safety culture. Awareness is as critical important for identifying threats, [root causes](/blog/how-to-conduct-root-cause-analysis-in-sms-programs), and other types of exposure before safety is compromised.

Organizations whose employees have good safety awareness will feature a high percentage of low-risk issues. This is because issues are often reported early in the hazard-risk lifecycle (i.e., low risk!). Furthermore, as management continues to identify and implement risk controls, they will become more aware of the best methods for controlling risk – this also leads to a reduced number of high/medium risk situations.

Hence, a great chart for monitoring the trend of safety awareness in your organization is the number of **hazard reports by risk level safety chart**. This chart shows:

You can also compare data to the previous time period for an even more thorough understanding of the context of current trends in safety awareness.

## 5 – Chart to Monitor Adaptability

![Responsiveness of issue closure chart](https://aviationsafetyblog.asms-pro.com/hubfs/images/Blog_Images/Responsiveness%20of%20issue%20closure%20chart.jpg "Responsiveness of issue closure chart")

Your organization’s ability to adapt to change is a significant marker of overall safety culture. By adopting, we are talking about responding to changes in the operational environment. Two great charts to monitor adaptability are:

Both charts show how quickly your SMS can do the “Four I’s”:

Once the problem has been fixed, the issue can be closed/reviewed, hence the average days to close the issue chart. Programs that are highly adaptable feature:

Adaptable aviation safety cultures should have low average days of close issues and a very high percentage of on-time CPA closures.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Utilizing interactive graphs and charts, such as pie and bar graphs, enhances visualization of safety data, facilitating trend identification and effective communication of insights.
Dashboards like those from SiteDocs Analytics provide real-time visualizations of safety metrics, allowing users to monitor compliance, incident rates, and training progress efficiently.
Geographic Information System (GIS) applications enable spatial analysis of safety data, helping organizations visualize risk patterns and resource allocation for enhanced safety management.
